title: Tourbillion v0.1.0
content:
Tourbillion opened as a new standalone browser screensaver app inside the JeffersonWM app family. This first pass focused on getting the imported project into a clean static shape, removing accidental baggage from the source package, and turning it into something we can actually extend and learn from.

What changed:

  • Installed Tourbillion under the JeffersonWM apps directory as a static front-end app intended for /tourbillion/, with its identity, title, build settings, and documentation rewritten around the new name.
  • Removed obvious AI Studio leftovers and unused scaffolding so the app behaves like a clean local screensaver project rather than an inherited generated export.
  • Began splitting the app into real modules by moving shared types and defaults into dedicated files, extracting the settings trigger, settings panel, and status overlay, and starting a dedicated trains mode module for train-specific helpers and initialization logic.
  • Added a functional Low / Medium / High quality system so the heavier train mode now has a real performance dial instead of a one-size-fits-all rendering load.
  • Hooked that quality setting into train-mode behavior by changing train population, seeded track length, smoke density, particle cap, and car-history density so larger displays can run a lighter version when needed.

The result is a strong opening baseline: Tourbillion is now installed, buildable, cleaner to reason about, and better prepared for future mode-specific tuning and visual changes.
